board.name: Japan Trip — Spring 2025
board.description: Two-week self-guided tour across Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka. Must book early for cherry blossom season.

tasks.name: Compare flight options
tasks.description: Check ANA, JAL, and Singapore Airlines. Look for Haneda arrival (closer to city). Budget target under $1200 round trip.

tasks.name: JR Pass cost analysis
tasks.description: Calculate if 14-day JR Pass is worth it vs single tickets. Need to account for Tokyo-Kyoto-Osaka shinkansen routes.

tasks.name: Book Tokyo hotel (5 nights)
tasks.description: Shinjuku or Shibuya area for first leg. Look for places with luggage forwarding service to Kyoto.

tasks.name: Book Kyoto ryokan (4 nights)
tasks.description: Want at least one night in a traditional ryokan with onsen. Check booking cancellation policies.

tasks.name: Reserve TeamLab tickets
tasks.description: Tickets sell out weeks in advance. Set reminder for when the next batch opens.

tasks.name: Book shinkansen reserved seats
tasks.description: Reserve seats on the Tokaido Shinkansen for the Tokyo to Kyoto leg. Window seat on the right side for Mt. Fuji view.

tasks.name: Check passport validity
tasks.description: Passport must be valid for entire stay. Renew now if expiring within 6 months of travel.

tasks.name: Purchase travel insurance
tasks.description: Look for plans covering medical evacuation and trip cancellation. Compare SafetyWing and World Nomads.

tasks.name: Download offline maps
tasks.description: Pre-download Google Maps offline for Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka. Also get Japan-specific navigation app.

tasks.name: Register for Visit Japan Web
tasks.description: Pre-clear customs and immigration online. Get QR codes ready before departure to skip paper forms.

tasks.name: Copy passports and store in cloud
tasks.description: Make digital copies of all passports, visas, and important documents. Store in encrypted cloud folder.

tasks.name: Plan Day 1-3 Tokyo highlights
tasks.description: Tsukiji outer market in morning, Senso-ji temple, Akihabara afternoon. Book Tsukiji sushi tour.

tasks.name: Plan Kyoto temple route
tasks.description: Optimize order: Fushimi Inari early morning (less crowded), then Kinkaku-ji, then Arashiyama bamboo grove.

tasks.name: Book omakase dinner
tasks.description: Look for Michelin-starred sushi counter. Reserve 2 months ahead through hotel concierge.

tasks.name: Find okonomiyaki spot in Osaka
tasks.description: Research Dotonbori area. Find a place with good reviews that takes reservations.

tasks.name: Research tsukemen shops in Tokyo
tasks.description: Make a shortlist of top-rated tsukemen shops near the hotel. Check if they have English menus.

tasks.name: Set daily spending limit
tasks.description: Target $150/day including food, transport, and activities. Exclude accommodation and flights.

tasks.name: Track deposits paid
tasks.description: Keep spreadsheet of all deposits for hotels, tours, and train reservations. Total so far: $2100.

tasks.name: Get travel adapter
tasks.description: Japan uses Type A/B plugs (same as US). Existing adapters work fine.

tasks.name: Learn basic Japanese phrases
tasks.description: Memorized arigato, sumimasen, gochisousama. Practiced ordering at a local izakaya.

tasks.name: Notify credit card companies
tasks.description: All cards have travel notices active. Confirmed no foreign transaction fees on any of them.
